sessions.intent: Restructure LazyParamsGenerator into OBP shape — extract per-property emission into a polymorphic ActionProperty hierarchy without changing emitted output.

before.assumptions: Source generator stays (no reflection, no runtime compilation). Round 1 targets the 11-way per-property if/else only; markers, ExecuteAsync, helpers stay procedural. LangVersion=latest is already set, so records-with-inheritance work.
before.risk: Byte-for-byte output drift if branch priority or AppendLine ordering changes. Roslyn incremental caching could break if record equality is sloppy. Both mitigated by Phase 0 regression harness (pin .g.cs output before refactor, diff after).

actions.context: Design plan for round 1 of LazyParamsGenerator OBP refactor. Discriminated three alternatives (pre-generated files on disk, runtime Roslyn compilation, reflection-based ActionInvoker) and ruled them all out per Ingi's direction. Settled on extracting per-property emission only, with markers and ExecuteAsync kept procedural. Verified LangVersion=latest on PLang.Generators.csproj before committing to records-with-inheritance.

after.status: design complete, plan approved, awaiting coder handoff
after.health: no code touched, no risk introduced, regression contract (byte-for-byte golden diff) defined upfront
after.notes: Architect role ends here. Ingi approved committing golden snapshot to .bot/. Recommended next bot: coder directly (test-designer optional — golden diff is the test). changes.patch empty since architect-only session.

sessions.intent: Fresh-eyes review of v1; combine structural OBP refactor with contract simplification (helpers move to ActionBase, runtime kind-switch, thin generated ExecuteAsync) so the property hierarchy is built once in its final shape. Replaces v1 byte-for-byte golden harness with a runtime test matrix as the regression contract.

before.assumptions: Directory-as-API is a project-wide convention (Ingi confirmed) — internal generator structure follows it consistently. C# partial classes union base declarations, so generator can declare ActionBase as base while user partial declares interfaces. No existing action handler under App/modules/ inherits from a non-interface base (verified via grep).
before.risk: Bigger PR than v1; byte-for-byte safety is gone in favor of a runtime test matrix that must comprehensively cover ParamKind × {nullability, default, type-class}. Phase 0 matrix design is the load-bearing risk control.

actions.context: v2 supersedes v1. Fresh-eyes review surfaced three concerns: (1) thirteen @this folders looked cosmetic in a non-navigated project — resolved when Ingi confirmed directory-as-API is a project-wide commitment for LLM maintenance; (2) v1 was the second-most-impactful work because round 2's contract simplification would partially dismantle round 1's structure; (3) per-property logic splits across 5+ iteration sites in GenerateActionCode, not 3 as v1 implied. Concerns 2 and 3 argued for combining the rounds. v2 builds the property hierarchy in its final small shape (records carrying IsMatch + BuildResolveCall + optional emission slots), moves all helpers to App/modules/ActionBase/this.cs, introduces ParamKind enum to drive runtime dispatch, and shrinks per-class generated code from ~200 to ~50 lines. Regression contract: runtime test matrix (15–20 minimal action handlers covering every ParamKind × variant), not byte-for-byte golden files.

sessions.intent: Re-review v2 in conversation; supersede with v3 that drops ActionBase, drops ParamKind dispatch, moves resolution onto Data, puts lookup/scaffolding on Action.@this, collapses property hierarchy to two leaves, enforces Data<T>-only handler properties at build time.

before.assumptions: Action.Parameters is List<Data> (verified). Data<T> inherits Data; Data is essentially Data<object> for routing. Data.As<T>(Context) already exists as the typed-view primitive (line 389 of App/Data/this.cs). NeedsResolution flag exists on Data. Variables.ResolveDeep does the recursive walk today, with a discarded-return-value bug on the typed-list branch. llm.query handler uses Data<List<LlmMessage>> with %var% nested inside Content — verified the deep-resolution case is real.
before.risk: Bigger contract change than v2 (which still kept ParamKind). Behavior shift in Data.Value getter (now does work lazily) could affect debugger/audit paths that read .Value for inspection. Cycle risk: Data.Value → Variables.Get → returns Data → reads its Value (mitigated by NeedsResolution=false on already-resolved Variable Data). Migration: any handler with non-Data<T> property must convert (likely small scope based on read.cs and llm/query.cs samples).

actions.context: v3 supersedes v2 after substantive design conversation. Three architectural shifts: (1) ActionBase dropped — helpers belong on Action.@this (the data owner); handlers stay flat with no synthetic base class. (2) Resolution moves to Data.@this — Data.Value lazy getter does the variable lookup and recursive %var% substitution; Variables.ResolveDeep evaporates. (3) Property dispatch dissolves entirely — every Data<T> property emits one uniform line: __action.GetParameter(name, Context).As<T>(Context). Hierarchy collapses from 10 leaves to 2 (DataProperty, ProviderProperty). Build-time validation enforces Data<T>-only properties; runtime trusts the .pr. Regression contract stays: runtime test matrix (~22-25 handlers covering kind × type-shape × resolution combinations). Plan walks llm.query end-to-end as the grounding example — including deep-nested %var% inside List<LlmMessage>.

sessions.intent: Pull runtime2 (Builder V2 + ~50 commits since branch split), assess v3 plan against the new state, and revise. Outcome: v4 supersedes v3 with one architectural sharpening — resolution moves from Data.Value getter to Data.As<T>(context); Data becomes stateless w.r.t. resolution.

before.assumptions: v3 plan structurally approved but not yet implemented. runtime2 had moved forward substantially during the gap — Builder V2 merged plus heavy iteration on Data caching (_resolved flag, _rawValue, ResetResolution, IsDeferredActionTemplate carve-out) and on the generator (per-action callstack/save-restore scaffolding, __SnapshotParams, ParamSnapshot diagnostic).
before.risk: Initial pull surfaced a build break on origin/runtime2 (duplicate finally in generator emission, duplicate Goal.RunAsync/AsData) — Ingi pushed a fix commit (00fd25e3) before I revised. Real risk: v3's choice to put resolution on Data.Value's getter is the same architectural decision the runtime2 patches were plastering over (commit chain f9008255 → 4c22c895 → b0014c91 → fedd44e6). v3 would inherit the same wound.

actions.context: v4 supersedes v3 after pulling runtime2's accumulated work. Walked the recent runtime2 patches around resolution (Data._resolved, _rawValue, ResetResolution, IsDeferredActionTemplate, generator-emitted data.ResetResolution() at every dispatch) and recognized them as a four-step retreat around one bad decision: putting resolution as a side effect on Data.Value's getter. v3 was about to repeat the same decision in different clothes (Phase 2: 'move ResolveDeep into Data.Value lazy getter'). The sharpening: resolution lives in Data.As<T>(context) — fresh walk + substitute + convert per call, returning Data<T>. The handler's __backing field caches the result for the call; ExecuteAsync resets backing fields per call. Data is stateless w.r.t. resolution. This dissolves the entire caching cluster on Data. On the open question (callers that read .Value on parameter Data expecting resolved form), Ingi's stance: 'if it is, then it's not valid implementation. Let it break and find out.' v4 plan: same structural skeleton as v3 (Action.GetParameter, App.Run scaffolding, generator hierarchy) but Phase 2 simplifies dramatically (write As<T>, delete plaster), Phase 3 emits no ResetResolution, Phase 4 grows by EmitSnapshotEntry on ActionProperty, Phase 6 confirms-deletion instead of slim-As<T>. Risks section shrinks (Data.Value side-effect risk and cycle risk are designed out). Matrix grows by 3 cases: DataWrappedActionList (verifies As<T> doesn't walk into Action), ConcurrentHandlers (verifies stateless Data), ReResolveAcrossCalls (verifies backing-field reset gets fresh resolution).
Merge runtime2 into local branch (committed locally, not pushed). Build green after Ingi's 00fd25e3 fix. No code modified beyond the merge itself; v4 design lives only in .bot/.

after.status: v4 plan written, awaiting Ingi's read-back and approval before implementation handoff
after.health: no code touched beyond the merge of origin/runtime2 (no conflicts, build green); design-only session
after.notes: Architect role ends here. Recommended next bot: test-designer to flesh out the matrix (~25-28 handlers with the three new cases), then coder for Phase 1 onwards. Phase 0 review point still recommended after matrix lands.

sessions.intent: Translate v4 plan into a comprehensive C# test suite — matrix handlers under PLang.Tests/Generator/Matrix/ plus contract tests for As<T> resolution, Data.Value raw contract, Action.GetParameter, App.Run scaffolding, generator validation, and __SnapshotParams.

before.assumptions: Per Ingi: no PLang .goal tests (existing PLang tests already exercise variable resolution end-to-end); coder builds the fixture/test helpers; tests encode v4's contract (some will fail before Phase 2 lands — intentional). Existing DataResolutionTests.cs encodes the OPPOSITE contract (snapshot-once .Value semantics) and will be rewritten as part of this plan.
before.risk: Matrix must cover every property kind × type-shape combination — gaps mean undetected drift through the refactor. The architect's enumeration is the starting point; my job is to expand it where the contract demands more coverage (e.g., concurrency, re-resolve across calls, snapshot-on-error).

actions.context: Matrix test classes — one per group, covering every property kind × type-shape combination from the architect's v4 Phase 0 enumeration. Each [Test] body is Assert.Fail('Not implemented'); intent comments above each method ARE the spec. Total ~73 matrix tests across 11 files. The corresponding handler stubs (StringPlain.cs etc.) are NOT created here — they need the source generator to run on PLang.Tests, and the analyzer reference is missing on PLang.Tests.csproj. Coder wires that up + builds handlers + fixture.

Generator-level contract tests asserting v4's source generator simplification: build-time enforcement that every action property is Data<T> or [Provider]; uniform property-emission shape (one shape per parameter property — collapses today's 11-arm dispatch); thin generated ExecuteAsync (no try/catch/finally — that lives in App.Run); per-property __SnapshotParams via ActionProperty.EmitSnapshotEntry. Directly maps to Ingi's mid-stream guidance: 'make source gen simpler.'

App-side contract tests for the v4 architectural pieces: Action.GetParameter (Phase 1), Data.As<T> as resolution entry point (Phase 2), Data.Value as raw read-only (Phase 2 negative-space contract), App.Run scaffolding wrapper (Phase 3). DataValueRawTests directly probes Ingi's big-picture frame: '.Value never does work; Data flows through unchanged; no _resolved/ResetResolution/IsDeferredActionTemplate.' GetParameterTests was originally placed under deep namespace PLang.Tests.App.Goals.Goal.Steps.Step.Actions.Action — caused namespace clash with global using Goal alias (broke SetupTests.cs build). Moved to flat PLang.Tests.App namespace; build green again.

Rewrote in place. The previous 276 lines encoded the OPPOSITE contract (snapshot-once .Value semantics — list/dict snapshots cached on first read, identity preserved across reads, NeedsResolution gating). Phase 2 of v4 deletes _resolved/ResetResolution/IsDeferredActionTemplate, so those tests would all fail. Replacement encodes v4's 'Data flows through, As<T> is fresh per call' integration scenarios: shared parameter Data across action executions, loop iteration freshness, sub-goal isolation, concurrent As<T> safety. 7 test methods (vs. previous ~14) — quality over quantity, each maps to a v4 contract assertion.
Bot output: plan.md (with mid-stream amendment for Ingi's 'Data flows through' big-picture clarification), v1 summary, bot-root summary, verdict pass.

after.status: Test suite complete — 139 test methods across 18 files (17 new + 1 rewritten in place). Build green.
after.health: All test bodies are Assert.Fail('Not implemented'); coder fills bodies as v4 phases land. Tests are designed to flip from failing → passing in phase order (Phase 1 flips GetParameterTests; Phase 2 flips DataValueRawTests + DataAsTResolutionTests + matrix Resolution/*; Phase 3 flips AppRunScaffoldingTests + matrix shape; etc.).
after.notes: Did NOT write matrix handler stubs (need PLang.Generators analyzer reference on PLang.Tests.csproj — coder wires it). Did NOT write fixture/test helpers (architect's plan defers to coder). Mid-stream message from Ingi reframed the goal as 'Data flows through, no unwrapping, source gen simpler' — incorporated into DataValueRawTests, DataAsTResolutionTests fast paths, GeneratorValidationTests uniform-shape assertions. Recommended next bot: coder for Phase 1 (Action.GetParameter) then Phase 2.

sessions.intent: Execute architect's v4 plan + fill in test-designer's 139 stub tests. Sequence: Phase 0 matrix handlers + fixture, Phase 1 Action.GetParameter, Phase 2 Data.As<T> + delete _resolved/ResetResolution cluster + ResolveDeep, Phase 3 App.Run scaffolding + new generator shape, Phase 4 generator hierarchy collapse, Phase 5 [VariableName] sweep + build-time validation, Phase 6 cleanup audit.

before.assumptions: Branch up to date with origin. Test suite (139 methods) all Assert.Fail. PLang.Generators not wired to PLang.Tests.csproj. Migration sweep is small (22 [VariableName] string properties; 250+ already Data<T>). Generator is single 779-line file with 11-arm property dispatch.
before.risk: Phase 2 is the contract change — code that reads .Value expecting resolution will break. Architect's gate: pause if >10 sites need fixing. ResolveDeep deletion may break OnResolveTrace subscribers (need to grep). Phase 3 scaffolding move from generated ExecuteAsync to App.Run requires interface tweak for __SnapshotParams handoff.

actions.context: Phase 0 — wired generator into PLang.Tests via Analyzer reference. ~28 matrix handler stubs in App.modules.matrix.* (parent App namespace resolves cleanly; not auto-discovered by Modules.Discover which walks PLang.dll only). MatrixRunner fixture registers handlers on demand and dispatches via App.Run. Generator emissions prefixed with global::App.* so they resolve in any consumer namespace (PLang.Tests's `global using App = App.@this` was shadowing the App namespace at sub-scope).
Phase 1 — Action.GetParameter(name, context) added. Pure lookup walking Parameters → Defaults → Data.NotFound. OrdinalIgnoreCase to match existing __Resolve patterns. 6 tests filled.

Phase 2 — the contract change. Rewrote Data.As<T>(context) as resolution entry point with substitution-before-fast-paths so T=object doesn't short-circuit. Action-destination carve-out preserves raw %var% in sub-action templates. Data.Value becomes raw read-only; deleted _resolved, _rawValue, NeedsResolution, ResetResolution(), IsDeferredActionTemplate. Variables.ResolveDeep + state + OnResolveTrace deleted. Generator stops emitting data.ResetResolution(). Plain Data.@this resolves via .As<object>(Context) so non-typed Data behaves like Data<object>. ZERO real prod breakage from contract change. 31 contract tests + 15 matrix Resolution tests filled.

Phase 3 — scaffolding (callstack push/pop, save/restore Step/Goal/Event, try/catch/finally with ServiceError translation, frame.SnapshotVariables) moved from generated ExecuteAsync into App.Run. ICodeGenerated gains SnapshotParams() so App.Run's catch can attach per-property diagnostics. Generated ExecuteAsync is now thin: marker init, eager Provider, backing reset, validation, return Run(). Generator stamps __<Name>_set in Data<T> getters so WasAccessed reads correctly. EngineTests.StepRunAsync_ExceptionInHandler updated: handler-exception now translates to ServiceError at App.Run, not StepError at Step. 8 scaffolding tests + 60 matrix tests filled.

Phase 4 — collapsed generator from 711-line LazyParamsGenerator.cs into Discovery/ + Emission/{Action,Property/{Data,Provider,Legacy}} hierarchy. ActionProperty abstract record exposes EmitProperty + EmitSnapshotEntry. Records use string/bool only — no IPropertySymbol leaks (Roslyn incremental safe). Discovery factory dispatches per-property (Provider/Data/Legacy). Snapshot emission moves to per-property. PLang.Tests.csproj enables EmitCompilerGeneratedFiles so structural tests can read .g.cs from disk. 13 GeneratorValidationTests + 8 SnapshotParamsTests filled.

Phase 5 — PLNG001 build-time diagnostic. Discovery emits a Roslyn diagnostic (DiagnosticSeverity.Error) when a partial action property is not Data<T>/plain Data, [Provider], or [VariableName] string. DiagnosticInfo flows through the IIncrementalGenerator pipeline as value-equal record. Manual smoke test confirmed PLNG001 fires on raw scalars. DEFERRED from architect plan: full [VariableName] deletion. Reason: variable.set/list.* handlers need the variable's NAME (not value) — As<T>(Context) flattens variable name into parameter property name. [VariableName] remains the cleanest expression of 'I want the name, not the value.' Logged in claude-md-proposals.md.

Phase 6 — audit + final wrap. Verified no leftover references to deleted APIs (ResolveDeep, ResetResolution, NeedsResolution, IsDeferredActionTemplate, _resolved, _rawValue, OnResolveTrace) anywhere in PLang/ or PlangConsole/. Cleaned up stale RawValue comment. PlangConsole builds. Wrote v1/summary.md and bot-root summary.md.

after.status: All 6 phases of architect v4 landed; [VariableName] removal deferred (documented). 2427/2427 tests passing.
after.health: Generator restructured into a clean hierarchy. Build green, full test suite green. PLNG001 diagnostic enforces v4 contract going forward. No real production breakage from the Data.Value contract change.
after.notes: Recommended next bot: codeanalyzer (review OBP/quality on Discovery + Emission), then auditor. Full [VariableName] removal is its own design pass — needs a way to preserve variable name through As<T>(Context) for variable.set/list.* handlers.
